title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

The world map displays the geographic distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, distinguishing between eligible and not eligible nations using a two-color scheme. Countries shaded in teal represent those eligible for support, while those in light beige are not eligible. The teal shading covers a contiguous block of nations across sub-Saharan Africa, extending from the western coast through the central region to the eastern horn and southern tip of the continent. In Asia, eligible countries include Yemen, Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, Myanmar, and several nations in Southeast Asia such as Cambodia and Papua New Guinea. A small cluster of eligible nations is also visible in the Caribbean and Central America. In contrast, the light beige color dominates North America, South America, Europe, Russia, China, Australia, and most of the Middle East and North Africa. The visual pattern indicates that eligibility is concentrated in lower-income regions, particularly across Africa and parts of South and Southeast Asia, while higher-income regions across the Americas, Europe, and East Asia fall outside the support criteria. The map provides a clear spatial representation of the income-based eligibility threshold, with the teal areas corresponding to countries meeting the Gross National Income per capita requirement over the specified three-year period.
